slide1: The length of a password is significantly more important than its complexity.
slide2: Passwords should be minimum 8 characters long, preferably at least 16 characters long.
slide3: Avoid using common, simple and easy to guess dictionary words in passwords.
slide4: Do not use repeating or predictable sequences in passwords (e.g., 'aaaa' or '1234').
slide5: Do not include personal information in passwords (e.g., birthdate, name, etc.).
slide6: Use password managers or other tools that provide similar functionality to safely store passwords.
slide7: Change your password immediately if a data breach occurs.
slide8: Do not reuse the same password across multiple systems, websites, etc.
slide9: Avoid using password recovery or reminder questions and hints.
slide10: Protect your logins by enabling two-factor authentication (2FA).
recommend: Improve your password security by following these recommendations
desc: Enter your password to check its strength and if it has appeared in data breaches
found: This password has been found in {{count}} data breaches.
notFound: This password has not been found in any known data breaches.
unable: Unable to check if password has been breached. Please try again later.
